nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for ClickHouse analytics patterns covering table design (MergeTree variants), query optimization, data insertion, materialized views, and common analytics queries such as time-series, funnel, and cohort analysis.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for creating structured `.tour` files that walk readers through a codebase; covers step types (file, selection, directory, URI, pattern), the SMIG writing rule, narrative shape, and anti-patterns.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Four-phase codebase onboarding skill (reconnaissance, architecture mapping, convention detection, artifact generation) that produces a human-readable onboarding guide and a starter CLAUDE.md for new contributors.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for integrating the CodeScene Code Health MCP server to measure maintainability scores, run feedback loops, and enforce quality gates using standalone tools only — with explicit AGENTS.md enforcement examples.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive coding-standards reference covering TypeScript/JavaScript naming, immutability, error handling, React patterns, REST API conventions, file organisation, JSDoc, performance (memoization, lazy loading), testing (AAA pattern), and code-smell detection.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for scoping and executing competitive platform analysis — establishing a client positioning brief, selecting competitors via taxonomy axes and scoring matrix, and producing a tiered shortlist with data-source guidance.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide prescribing an 8-section competitive report structure (executive summary through methodology appendix) with framing principles, presentation rules, decision frameworks, and team-alignment trigger questions.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for Kotlin Compose Multiplatform patterns covering ViewModel state management, event-sink pattern, type-safe navigation, slot-based composable APIs, platform-specific expect/actual, performance (stable types, derivedStateOf), and Material 3 theming.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for auditing and garbage-collecting stale Claude Code configuration — scanning permissions, hooks, env vars, and MCP configs across global and project settings to surface dead entries safely.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step installation and configuration guide for ECC (Everything Claude Code), covering clone, installation-level selection, skill/rule selection and install, post-installation verification, optional optimisation, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for scoring and pruning social-network connections (X/Twitter and LinkedIn) using a scoring model across three modes (light-pass, default, aggressive), with platform-specific rules and a structured review-pack format.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for source-first content repurposing across X, LinkedIn, short video, YouTube, and newsletter, with voice-handling rules, hard bans on AI clichés, platform adaptation rules, and a quality gate checklist.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for implementing a content-hash–based file cache using Python frozen dataclasses, SHA-256 cache keys, file-based storage, and a service-layer wrapper following SRP — with explicit key design decisions and anti-patterns.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for auditing an LLM session's context window budget — inventorying token consumers, classifying them by importance, detecting overuse patterns, and generating a prioritised report for pruning.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Minimal skill guide for selecting and combining continuous agent loop patterns (CronCreate vs Monitor tool) based on whether the next trigger is time-based or event-based, including failure modes and recovery steps.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Runtime configuration for the continuous-learning-v2 skill, specifying the version and observer settings used by the observe.sh hook.

nodes.name: observe.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code hook script that triggers the continuous-learning observer on tool events — resolves the correct Python binary, manages observer lifecycle (start/check/restart), and routes hook payloads to instinct-cli.py.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses UPPERCASE function names for hook-internal helpers (_REMOVE_FILE_IF_PRESENT, _START_OBSERVER_LOGGED, _CHECK_OBSERVER_RUNNING), following the ECC hook naming convention.

nodes.name: detect-project.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that identifies the current project's root and remote URL, normalises the remote URL to a canonical form, and updates the continuous-learning project registry so the observer can scope instincts correctly.

nodes.name: instinct-cli.py
nodes.summary: Core Python CLI for the continuous-learning-v2 skill that manages instinct storage across projects and global scope — supports status, import, export, evolve, promote, prune, and project management subcommands.
nodes.languageNotes: 1914-line monolithic Python CLI using stdlib only (no third-party deps); uses fcntl for file locking on registry writes and subprocess for git operations.

nodes.name: homunculus-dir.sh
nodes.summary: Tiny shell library that resolves and exports the homunculus storage directory path, respecting the CLAUDE_HOMUNCULUS_DIR override env var and defaulting to XDG_DATA_HOME or ~/.local/share.

nodes.name: migrate-homunculus.sh
nodes.summary: One-shot migration script that moves legacy homunculus data from the old ~/.claude/homunculus directory to the new XDG-compliant storage path used by continuous-learning-v2.

nodes.name: test_parse_instinct.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Python test suite for the instinct-cli.py parsing and core utility functions, covering parse_instinct_file, URL validation, project detection helpers, and registry operations.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for the continuous-learning-v2 system that automatically captures, stores, and evolves per-project coding instincts from Claude Code sessions — covers architecture, instinct lifecycle (observe → store → evolve → promote), and CLI usage.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for building cost-aware LLM pipelines using content-hash caching, model tier selection, and batching strategies to reduce redundant API calls and optimise token spend.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guide for tracking and reporting LLM API costs within a project, covering token metering, cost aggregation by model tier, and reporting formats for surfacing spend to developers.

nodes.name: detect_project
nodes.summary: Detects the current project by resolving git repo root and remote URL, computes a stable project hash, migrates legacy storage if needed, creates project directories, and updates the project registry.

nodes.name: parse_instinct_file
nodes.summary: Parses a raw instinct file (YAML-like key-value + prose content blocks) into a list of structured instinct dictionaries with confidence, domain, and content fields.

nodes.name: cmd_status
nodes.summary: Implements the 'status' subcommand — detects current project, loads all instincts, displays counts by scope and domain, reports pending observations, and warns about legacy data.

nodes.name: cmd_import
nodes.summary: Implements the 'import' subcommand — fetches instincts from a URL or file, deduplicates against existing instincts, prompts for confirmation, writes accepted entries to the project instinct directory, and removes stale predecessors.

nodes.name: cmd_export
nodes.summary: Implements the 'export' subcommand — loads project instincts, serialises them as YAML-like files to a specified output directory for sharing or backup.

nodes.name: cmd_evolve
nodes.summary: Implements the 'evolve' subcommand — collects pending observations and candidate instincts, calls _generate_evolved to synthesise new/updated instincts via LLM, and writes results back.

nodes.name: cmd_promote
nodes.summary: Dispatcher for the 'promote' subcommand, routing to either _promote_specific (single instinct by ID) or _promote_auto (batch promotion of cross-project instincts).

nodes.name: cmd_projects
nodes.summary: Implements the 'projects' subcommand — lists all registered projects with their instinct counts, supporting delete, gc, and merge sub-operations via delegated helpers.

nodes.name: cmd_prune
nodes.summary: Implements the 'prune' subcommand — removes low-confidence or stale instincts from project and global stores, with dry-run support.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point for instinct-cli.py — parses sys.argv, dispatches to the appropriate cmd_* handler, and handles top-level error reporting.

nodes.name: load_all_instincts
nodes.summary: Loads and merges instincts from both project-local and global storage, deduplicating by ID and applying precedence rules so project-specific instincts shadow global ones.

nodes.name: _generate_evolved
nodes.summary: Synthesises evolved instinct files from skill candidates, workflow instincts, and agent candidates by composing prompts and writing structured YAML output to the evolved directory.
